behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real model output quality from the speech-to-text API (speaker diarization/label retention). A mocked transcript would hide diarization failures (MOCK-HIDE). No real multi-agent sequence is required if we assert on STT output artifact itself, so Workflow is rejected: behavior text is specifically about "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels," not full multi-step agent handoff correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run STT on real multi-speaker meeting audio with known speaker turns; assert transcript preserves speaker labels/segments well enough for downstream attendee attribution (e.g., each expected speaker appears with correctly labeled utterances).

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic over transcript text to structured fields; primary failure is local extraction/normalization rules, not OS/network/model behavior (MOCK-MISS indicates function/object boundary). Integration was considered and rejected because behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff; it focuses on "parsing extracts ... into the summary input structure."
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test parser with crafted transcript snippets (clear assignments, ambiguous phrasing, multiple assignees, punctuation/noise) and assert exact assignee/task objects in the produced summary-input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output decisions (coverage/completeness of action items per attendee). Stubbed responses would trivially pass and miss omission failures (LLM-DEP + deterministic-assertion trap). Workflow was considered and rejected because behavior text targets summary-output quality, not an explicit multi-agent sequence contract.
behaviors.test_description: Feed realistic transcripts with known attendee assignments to the real summarization model and assert attendee-level recall: each attendee with an assignment appears with at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic routing/serialization logic after decisions are already identified; failure is local mapping logic. No real infra or model judgment is required. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: "routes identified decisions" implies identification is out of scope.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test routing mapper with pre-identified decision objects and mixed narrative content; assert decision entries are emitted only in `decisions` schema fields and excluded from generic narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This tests model extraction/placement quality from transcript to summary sections. Mocking model output would hide real misses and misplacement (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Workflow was considered and rejected because behavior text is about one summary outcome from transcript, not explicit validation of multiple agentic handoff stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts containing explicit decisions; assert expected decisions are present in `decisions` and not only in freeform narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing across segments plus merged coherence. This chains multiple agentic calls (per-segment STT/summarization) and cross-step state/handoff correctness (MULTI-STEP). Lower levels with stubs can verify orchestration mechanics but can miss coherence/regression caused by real model outputs across segments.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute meeting recording; run end-to-end segmented processing; assert segment boundaries are respected, all segments are processed in order, and merged output preserves cross-segment action items/decisions/attributions without duplication or contradiction.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit full-pipeline execution over real recording with multiple agentic stages (STT then LLM summarization) plus delivery orchestration. Failures can arise from cross-stage handoff/state, external APIs, and model behavior; therefore Workflow is the minimum real-failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like run with a real meeting file: upload, real transcription, real summarization, and final structured output delivery; assert successful completion, schema validity, and no human retries/manual edits.
